post.description:
Return a list of cases according to a given status.
The following statuses are available:

Created: a list of cases create and not assigned yet;

Assigned: a list of cases assigned to the clinician waiting for review;

Waiting: a list of cases assigned to the clinician, waiting for the patient information or review;

Cancelled: a list of cases assigned to the clinician and canceled by the partner;

Support: a list of cases assigned to the clinician and transferred to support;

Processing: a list of cases assigned to the clinician that were already reviewed and finished by the clinician, but with one or more prescriptions waiting for DoseSpot confirmation;

Completed: a list of cases assigned to the clinician with all its prescriptions already confirmed by DoseSpot (prescriptions already verified by the pharmacies).

data.text:
*****
For any non-medical questions (such as account questions, billing, shipping, etc.) please reach out to ScoreBlue support directly at: support@ScoreBlue.com as support would be the ones to handle those questions.  
*****


Hi Mark , I’m Dr. Thomas Bottoni MD , a board certified physician who is licensed in Florida.  I’ve reviewed your health information and I think you likely have ED, which can benefit from the following:   Sildenafil 20mg tablets.  Start with 1-2 tablets and do not exceed a maximum of 100 mg of sildenafil (5 tablets) per day based on how you respond to treatment.

The treatment plan including other important information is below  Please make sure you review it, as it will help you decide if the benefits of the plan outweigh the risks.

Don’t forget that there is always a package insert that comes with your prescription which you should review.

Please feel free to contact me here directly if you have any medical questions.

Sincerely,
Dr. Thomas Bottoni MD 
Board-certified physician licensed in Florida 

How to use the medication: Start with two tablets of the medication by mouth for your first time one hour before sex as needed.  You may then go up by 1 tablet each future sexual encounter if you feel you need to.  DO NOT TAKE MORE THAN FIVE TABLETS AT A TIME OR WITHIN A 24 HOUR PERIOD.

Do not use this in combination with any illegal drugs. Taking with alcohol may make the medication less effective and increase the chances of side effects such as headache or flushing. Never take erectile dysfunction medication with nitrates for chest pain.

SIDE EFFECTS: Here's your reference chart - don't panic if you experience these. They're ultra-normal. Most side effects only last as long as the medication is still in the system. That means it's a shorter duration for Viagra, Sildenafil, and Levitra...but longer for Cialis.
 
Symptom				Frequency
* Headache                        16-28% vs 7% for placebo                
* Upset stomach               10-29% vs 2% for placebo
* Flushing                           10-29% vs 2% for placebo
* Stuffy nose                       4% vs 2% for placebo
* Dizziness.                         3-4% vs 2% for placebo
* Muscle aches                   3-4% vs 2% for placebo
* Dyspepsia (‘reflux’)         3-17% vs 2% for placebo

Less common side effects include:
* Rash
* Abnormal vision (blue tint, blurry vision light sensitivity)

Severe Side Effects (very uncommon)
* Erections that last several hours. Tell your doctor right away if you have an erection that lasts more than 4 hours.
* Decreased eyesight or loss of sight in one or both eyes.
* Sudden decrease or loss of hearing.
* Heart attack, stroke, irregular heartbeats, and death. Most of these happened in people who already had heart problems.
* Seizures
* Low blood pressure causing dizziness
* Allergic reaction
* Stevens-Johnson syndrome
* Anemia
If any of these SEVERE side effects occur, seek medical care immediately.

We require that you continue to follow up regularly (at least once a year) with your primary care doctor for in person check ups. It is important that you inform them of your ED symptoms and treatment so that they can investigate any additional contributors and optimize treatment for any underlying conditions/triggers which may include: heart disease, atherosclerosis, high cholesterol, high blood pressure, diabetes, obesity, metabolic syndrome, Parkinson’s disease, multiple sclerosis, sleep apnea, prostate disease, other GU diseases, low testosterone, psychological conditions, certain medications, smoking, drug and alcohol use.  Please remember to let us know of any changes in your health. If you have any anatomic problems or urology surgeries that could impact erectile dysfunction or if there is any change to your cardiovascular health please always let myself and your primary doctor know as this may impact the treatment recommendations for your ED.

FOR OTHER HELPFUL INFORMATION INCLUDING ALTERNATIVES TO THERAPY, please read the following: https://support.scoreblue.com/hc/en-us  

If you do not have a PCP, please click on the following link: https://www.google.com/search?q=primary+care+doctor+near+me

user.bio_details:
Maura J. Holcomb, M.D. is the author of more than 60 papers on clinical interests that include psoriasis, acne, melanoma, tropical dermatology, and photosensitive skin disorders. Her work has been published in the International Journal of Dermatology, Dermatology Online Journal, Journal of Cutaneous Pathology, and other dermatological publications, including numerous Continuing Medical Education articles for ClinicalAdvisor.com’s Dermatology Clinic. Dr. Holcomb has also collaborated on several presentations of dermatological research at conferences of the Texas Dermatological Society, American Society of 
 dermatopathology, and American Academy of Dermatology.

A native Texan, Dr. Holcomb graduated at the top of her high school class in East Texas before attending Texas A&M University, where she graduated magna cum laude, obtaining degrees in
both biology and psychology. 

Dr. Holcomb received her M.D. from Baylor College of Medicine on a full scholarship. She completed her medical internship at The Methodist Hospital in Houston and her dermatology residency at Baylor. She conducted clinical research at the Department of Dermatology at The University of Texas M.D. Anderson Cancer Center, Houston, and received the Debra Chester Kalter Award for Outstanding Student Research in Dermatology. Graduating in the top 10% of her class, she was elected into the Alpha Omega Alpha Medical Honor Society.

During medical school, Dr. Holcomb spent two months volunteering at a leprosy hospital in Nepal. In her spare time, she enjoys hiking, running, camping, travelling off the beaten path, and scuba diving.

Create a new encounter for a given patient.
Payload



field
type
description




patient_id
uuid*
Patient ID. Check Partners > Cases > Patients to create new patients


reference_case_id
uuid
The referenced case UUID


metadata
string(255)
Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the case. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this case in another database)


clinician_id
uuid*
Clinician to assign the case to, in case there is a preference for a specific clinician in a specific case. If the field is not sent, a clinician will be assigned according to MDIntegrations algorithm.


case_prescriptions
array(Prescription)
List of prescriptions to be reviewed by a clinician


case_services
array(Service)
List of services to be reviewed by a clinician


case_files
array(uuid)
Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check Partners > Files


case_questions
array(Question)
Questions and answers list


is_chargeable
boolean
Whether or not the case should be charged


tags
array(object(uuid))
Tags ID to be attached to the case.



Prescription Properties
Important: a prescription object must always contain exactly one drug type ID from the following:
[partner_medication_id, partner_compound_id, partner_supply_id, dosespot_supply_ndc]
Note: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly.



field
type
description




partner_compound_id
uuid
Partner compound ID. Check Partners > Compounds to get a partner_compound_id for a given compound. Required if dosespot_medication_id and partner_supply_id and dosespot_supply_ndc are null


partner_medication_id
uuid
Partner medication ID. It can be found on your company's dashboard. Required if dosespot_medication_id and partner_medication_id and partner_supply_id and dosespot_supply_ndc are null


partner_supply_id
uuid
Partner supply ID. It can be found on your company's dashboard. Required if dosespot_medication_id and partner_compound_id and partner_medication_id and dosespot_supply_ndc are null


dosespot_supply_ndc
string
Dosespot Supply NDC code. Required if dosespot_medication_id and partner_compound_id and partner_medication_id and partner_supply_id are null


refills
integer (from 0 to 99)
Total of refills for the given medication * Required if dosespot_medication_id or dosespot_supply_ndc is present


quantity
string
Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) Required if dosespot_medication_id or dosespot_supply_ndc is present


days_supply
integer (from 0 to 365)
Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows null value


directions
string(140)
Instructions about how the medication is administered Required if dosespot_medication_id or dosespot_supply_ndc is present


pharmacy_notes
string(210)
Additional information about the prescription


no_substitutions
boolean
Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed


dispense_unit_id
integer
- Medication (prescription with a dosespot_medication_id included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication;    - Compound (prescription with partner_compound_id included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check Partners > Dispense Units. * Required if dosespot_medication_id or dosespot_supply_ndc is present


pharmacy_id
integer
Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies to get a list of all pharmacies available.    Important note:    Dosespot works with different IDs for pharmacies according to the environment.  Example:  - Curexa pharmacy ID in staging environment: 133012  - Curexa pharmacy ID in production environment: 29992


effective_date
string
Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: Y-m-d



Prescription Status Flow
pending: When prescription is created on MDI side and not synced with Dosespot yet;processing: When MDI receives the Dosespot ID and send through the Dosespot the prescription to Pharmacy;completed: When Dosespot responds to the MDI that this prescription has been verified by the pharmacy;cancelled: When a doctor cancel the prescription (when it was synced with Dosespot we try to cancel it there too)

post.description:
With this endpoint, you can exchange your Client ID and Client Secret for an access token with limited lifetime.
The access token allows your application to make authenticated requests to the MD Integrations API.
For more details on the expected flow, you can checkout the Authentication section in the Introduction.
Payload
For this request, all fields in the payload are required.



field
type
description




grant_type
string*
Should always be client_credentials


client_id
string*
Client ID credential


client_secret
string*
Client Secret credential


scope
string*
Should always be \*



Expired tokens
Whenever an access token expires, you should request a brand new one for making new requests.
Client Credentials
Your credentials will be provided to you by the MD Integrations team.
Keep in mind the importance of protecting your credentials to avoid unauthorized access.

This event will be dispatched when a case has been created.
https://your.api.endpoint/ = Your API endpoint previously registered on our platform.
Secret Key = Your Secret Key previously registered on our platform.
The request will have a header called Signature created by Secret Key that will contain a signature the receiving app can use to verify the payload hasn't been tampered with.
To create the Signature we use the Secret Key as a salt with SHA256 following this example below:
$signature = hash_hmac('sha256', json_encode(payload), secret);
Authorization = Your Authorization previously registered on our platform.
The request will have a header called Authorization used to authenticate to your API

post.description:
Return a list of cases according to a given status.
The following statuses are available:

Created: a list of cases create and not assigned yet;

Assigned: a list of cases assigned to the clinician waiting for review;

Waiting: a list of cases assigned to the clinician, waiting for the patient information or review;

Cancelled: a list of cases assigned to the clinician and canceled by the partner;

Support: a list of cases assigned to the clinician and transferred to support;

Processing: a list of cases assigned to the clinician that were already reviewed and finished by the clinician, but with one or more prescriptions waiting for DoseSpot confirmation;

Completed: a list of cases assigned to the clinician with all its prescriptions already confirmed by DoseSpot (prescriptions already verified by the pharmacies).

Prescription Properties
Important: a prescription object must always contain exactly one drug type ID from the following:
[partner_medication_id, partner_compound_id, partner_supply_id, dosespot_supply_ndc]
Note: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly.



field
type
description




partner_compound_id
uuid
Partner compound ID. Check Partners > Compounds to get a partner_compound_id for a given compound. Required if dosespot_medication_id and partner_supply_id and dosespot_supply_ndc are null


partner_medication_id
uuid
Partner medication ID. ItIt can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. Required if dosespot_medication_id and partner_medication_id and partner_supply_id and dosespot_supply_ndc are null


partner_supply_id
uuid
Partner supply ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. Required if dosespot_medication_id and partner_compound_id and partner_medication_id and dosespot_supply_ndc are null


dosespot_supply_ndc
string
Dosespot Supply NDC code. Required if dosespot_medication_id and partner_compound_id and partner_medication_id and partner_supply_id are null


refills
integer (from 0 to 99)
Total of refills for the given medication * Required if dosespot_medication_id or dosespot_supply_ndc is present


quantity
string
Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) Required if dosespot_medication_id or dosespot_supply_ndc is present


days_supply
integer (from 0 to 365)
Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows null value


directions
string(140)
Instructions about how the medication is administered Required if dosespot_medication_id or dosespot_supply_ndc is present


pharmacy_notes
string(210)
Additional information about the prescription


no_substitutions
boolean
Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed


dispense_unit_id
integer
- Medication (prescription with a dosespot_medication_id included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication;    - Compound (prescription with partner_compound_id included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check Partners > Dispense Units. * Required if dosespot_medication_id or dosespot_supply_ndc is present


pharmacy_id
integer
Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies to get a list of all pharmacies available.    Important note:    Dosespot works with different IDs for pharmacies according to the environment.  Example:  - Curexa pharmacy ID in staging environment: 133012  - Curexa pharmacy ID in production environment: 29992


effective_date
string
Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: Y-m-d



Prescription Status Flow
pending: When prescription is created on MDI side and not synced with Dosespot yet;processing: When MDI receives the Dosespot ID and send through the Dosespot the prescription to Pharmacy;completed: When Dosespot responds to the MDI that this prescription has been verified by the pharmacy;cancelled: When a doctor cancel the prescription (when it was synced with Dosespot we try to cancel it there too)
